title: Analytics - Firmware Update Failed
scenario: Firmware Update Failed Firebase page does not display updated event counts or expected default elements (Device PID, Charge Unit PID, Update Type, Error Description) after a DFU failure
steps: 1. Open the Poly Studio Mobile app on the Android device.
2. Navigate to the Home screen and ensure a Poly Bluetooth audio device is connected.
3. Go to Poly Studio Mobile → [Device Name] → Device Settings.
4. Initiate a firmware update (DFU) for the connected device.
5. Cause the firmware update to fail (e.g., disconnect the device, interrupt the update process, or trigger an error condition).
6. Open the Firebase console and navigate to the Firmware Update Failed event page.

7. Verify the page loads properly and contains all expected default elements: Graph, Events in last 30 minutes, Event count by Country, Event count by Gender, Events per session, App Version, App build, App Environment.
8. Verify the page also contains the additional elements: Device PID, Charge Unit PID, Update Type, and Error Description.
9. Wait a few hours for event counts to update.
10. Revisit the Firmware Update Failed Firebase page and verify that the event count for the related elements (Device PID, Charge Unit PID, Update Type, Error Description) has increased.
expected: The Firmware Update Failed Firebase page loads successfully with all default elements (Graph, Events in last 30 minutes, Event count by Country, Event count by Gender, Events per session, App Version, App build, App Environment) and additional elements (Device PID, Charge Unit PID, Update Type, Error Description). After a few hours, the event counts for these elements increase, reflecting the failed DFU.
